political.s: <cite index="2-1,2-16">The Hershey PAC is bipartisan and distributes funds equally between both major political parties at federal and state levels.</cite> Your government data shows $99K raised with 13,000 to 16,000 rep%, consistent with the company's stated bipartisan approach. <cite index="2-13,2-14">Hershey does not make political contributions using corporate funds and only engages in political giving through Hershey's Political Action Committee (PAC), with funds made up of voluntary employee contributions.</cite>

charity.s: <cite index="1-1">Hershey's "Cocoa For Good" initiative is a 10-year, $500 million investment toward improving farming conditions, eliminating child labor, addressing children's nutrition, and protecting the environment.</cite> <cite index="8-1,8-2">The Hershey Company donated more than $9 million to charitable causes in 2012, with employees volunteering more than 200,000 hours in their local communities.</cite> <cite index="11-13">In 2024, Hershey provided over 4.1 million pounds of product to children and families in need.</cite>
charity.focus: Cocoa farming communities, child labor elimination, children's education/nutrition, community development

environment.s: <cite index="27-1,27-2">Hershey's target to reduce scope 1 and 2 GHG emissions by 50% by 2030 from a 2018 baseline was re-validated by the Science Based Targets initiative (SBTi), and to date, Hershey has achieved a 43% reduction against this target.</cite> <cite index="21-17">In 2024, Hershey exceeded its goal of reducing absolute water consumption by 20%, against a 2018 baseline, in priority facilities where water is most scarce.</cite> However, critics note ongoing child labor and deforestation concerns in cocoa sourcing despite these commitments.

labor.s: <cite index="7-3,7-4">Hershey has continued to improve its workplace practices and launched a "Manufacturing Apprenticeship Program" to recruit, train, and retain employees with physical or intellectual disabilities for its manufacturing plants.</cite> However, <cite index="7-14,7-15,7-16">although Hershey strives to be a model company, it has struggled with ethical issues related to labor exploitation in West African cocoa communities, including child labor, and critics argue Hershey is not doing enough to stop labor exploitation on cocoa plantations.</cite>

dei.s: <cite index="43-3,43-4,43-5">DiversityInc named The Hershey Co. No. 3 on its Top 50 Companies for Diversity annual list, with efforts guided by the Pathways Project, a five-year plan to make Hershey more diverse and inclusive.</cite> <cite index="43-11,43-13">Hershey's board of directors consists of 58 percent diverse representation, with 1:1 gender pay equity achieved in 2019 and 1:1 aggregate people of color pay equity for salaried employees achieved in 2021.</cite>

animals.s: <cite index="55-1">Hershey does not conduct, fund or contribute to animal testing.</cite> However, <cite index="51-2,51-4">when chocolate giant The Hershey Company provided cocoa ingredients and data analysis for an experiment in which mice were killed, it violated its own policy—which had been put in place after earlier discussions with PETA—not to conduct or fund animal testing to make health claims about its products.</cite> <cite index="55-2">Hershey committed to sourcing 100 percent certified cage-free eggs for the U.S. and Canadian markets from cage-free farms.</cite>

guns.s: Not applicable—Hershey is a confectionery and snacks company with no connection to firearms manufacturing or sales.

privacy.s: <cite index="71-5,71-7,71-8">Hershey is a participating member in the CARU Safe Harbor Program and has voluntarily undertaken privacy initiatives subject to audits and frequent monitoring of its websites.</cite> <cite index="71-17,71-18">Hershey may "sell" and "share" certain categories of personal data to third parties, though consumers may exercise opt-out rights.</cite> The company maintains detailed privacy policies but shares data with service providers and third parties for marketing purposes.

execPay.s: <cite index="69-1,69-5">Hershey disclosed its CEO pay was 281 times its median employee's pay for the fiscal year ending in 2024.</cite> <cite index="65-11,65-12">As Chairman of the Board, President and CEO, Michele G. Buck made $11,910,926 in total compensation, comprised of $1,400,000 in salary, $1,052,800 in bonus, and $9,027,171 in stock awards.</cite>

fec.committeeName: THE HERSHEY COMPANY POLITICAL ACTION COMMITTEE

sec.note: Pay ratio extracted from proxy filing — verify manually for accuracy
